short: Techniques that let a model handle prompts longer than its original training length.
definition: Context extension covers methods that let a model work with prompts longer than the context window — the maximum amount of text it can consider at once — that it was trained on. Common approaches adjust how the model tracks the position of each word, so it still keeps its bearings over longer stretches of text, sometimes with a little extra training. It matters because larger contexts let a model take in more documents or history at once.
